name: Polyphenol-rich diet increased serum indole-3-propionic acid in older adults with preserved kidney function
summary: The polyphenol diet trial adds older-adult human target engagement for a microbial tryptophan metabolite.
statement: In a 51-participant older-adult randomized crossover trial, an 8-week polyphenol-rich diet significantly increased serum indole-3-propionic acid only in participants with preserved kidney function, while IPA variation was associated with C-reactive protein and gut-microbiota order shifts.
population_or_model: Adults aged at least 60 years living in a residential care facility
quantitative_note: The abstract reports significant IPA increase in the normal kidney-function subgroup, association with C-reactive protein beta = 0.32, p = 0.010, and association with Clostridiales r = 0.35, p < 0.001.
caveats: The effect was subgroup-dependent and biomarker-level.
The intervention was a polyphenol-rich diet, not direct IPA replacement.
The study did not establish functional, cognitive, disease, or mortality benefit.
